title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the average velocity of the blast wave, we need to know the distance it traveled and the time it took.

title: Calculate the Distance
text: The blast wave traveled at an angle of 10° above the horizon. Use trigonometry to find the horizontal distance traveled.

title: Using Trigonometry
text: The horizontal distance can be calculated using the formula: distance $$=$$ $$\frac{altitude}tan^$\left(angle\righ$$.

title: Calculate the Time
text: Convert the time from minutes and seconds to seconds.

title: Time Conversion
text: The total time in seconds is: $$2$$ minutes $$30$$ seconds $$=$$ $$2\times60+30$$ seconds.

title: Average Velocity Formula
text: The average velocity is calculated using the formula: velocity $$=$$ $$\frac{distance}{time}$$.

title: Calculate Average Velocity
text: Substitute the values into the formula: velocity $$=$$ $$\frac{\frac{23.5}tan^{150}\left(10\right)$$.
